observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a) Create a new Inventory Committee to conduct a complete annual physical
count, including assignment of property number stickers to all property, plant,
and equipment of the Municipality to prove their existence, determine the
condition and the valuation, and prepare the RPCPPE;
b) Direct the GSO and Municipal Accountant to reconcile the latest inventory
report with the PPELC, which shall serve as the basis of the Inventory
Committee as basis in the preparation of the PIP;
c) Implement the procedures prescribed under COA Circular 2020-006 dated
January 31, 2020, in the conduct of physical count of PPE, recognition of PPE
items found at the station, disposition of non-existing/missing PPE items, and
derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs without an available record of
accountability, for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances of
government agencies to produce a reliable report and PPE balances;
d) Designate LGU personnel from the accounting and property offices who shall
maintain and update the PPE Ledger/Property Cards and regularly reconcile
their records, as well as maintain an updated ARE and safekeep all documents
relative to the acquisition of properties; and
e) Direct the GSO to secure and ensure that the AREs are completely and correctly
filled out with necessary details such as brand name, color, model, applicable
serial number, and other important information about the property.

We recommended that Management:
a) Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the
GSO with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for
road projects;
b) Require the Inventory Committee to conduct an inventory of local roads,
highways, and bridges within its area of jurisdiction which were acquired
through purchase, construction, or donations;
c) Direct the Municipal Accountant to record all road networks and their
components, based on the Registry of Public Infrastructures, or in its absence,
based on the complete Local Road Inventory Report provided with a complete
description and cost segregation of the road components;
d) Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Local Road Network Ledger
Card in the prescribed form shown in Annex D of COA Circular No. 2015-008
and to disclose the total road network system in the Notes to Financial
Statements;
e) Direct the GSO to maintain an updated Local Road Inventory and Road Map
and keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card of all roads within its
area of jurisdiction; and
f) Utilize geotagging technology to enhance the accuracy and accessibility of the
local road inventory and mapping.

We recommended the Acting Municipal Accountant to record the completed
project/s amounting to ₱32,961,264.92 to the appropriate PPE or equity account and
make the necessary adjusting entries on the unrecorded depreciation expense.

We recommended that Management:
a) Update the Inventory Committee to oversee and conduct a semi-annual
physical count of the inventory items;
b) Require the Inventory Committee to conduct timely counts of Inventory
items based on the timing prescribed by the Commission, and ensure proper
and complete documentation of the results of the count to facilitate reliable
reconciliation between the GSO records and the Accounting Office records;
c) Direct the GSO to submit to the Accounting Department corresponding
report of receipt and issuances of inventories for proper and timely
recording in the books of accounts;
d) Direct the concerned offices to provide the necessary utilization reports,
particularly for the long outstanding inventories, to the Office of the
Municipal Accountant and subsequently, the latter review the details of the
affected accounts, determine the status of the related inventories, and make
appropriate adjustments, if warranted;
e) Ensure the preparation of RPCI, SSMI, and SCs to facilitate reliable
reconciliation between accounting and property records;
f) Require the coordination between the Municipal Accountant and the GSO
towards the implementation of the Perpetual Inventory Method in the
recording of supplies and materials and maintaining the prescribed records
to ensure proper accounting and promote better control in the receipt and
issuance of supplies;
g) To provide, if possible, a secured storage room for the supplies and materials
of the LGU;
h) Coordinate with the concerned departments/offices and develop internal
control procedures that will ensure proper management of inventories to
prevent or minimize the risk of loss and misuse; and
i) Ensure compliance with the provisions of Republic Act No. 9184 and its
Implementing Rules and Regulations and Section 122 of the Manual on the
NGAS for LGUs for the procurement of supplies and materials.

We recommended that the Management:
a. Direct the Accountable Officers (AOs), through the issuance of a demand
letter, to submit the liquidation report together with the supporting
documents, to settle his/her cash advance; otherwise, require the immediate
return of the amount of cash advance that remained unliquidated or cause
the withholding of the payment of the AO’s salary;
b. Coordinate with the head of the other government agencies to which the
accountable officers are currently employed or connected to immediately
settle their cash advances;
c. Stop the grant of additional cash advances to personnel with unliquidated
cash advances until such time previous cash advances are fully settled;
d. Send demand letters to the accountable officers previously connected with
the LGU who still have unliquidated cash advances, otherwise officials
responsible for clearing said individuals from money accountability be held
liable for the unsettled cash advances; and
e. Monitor and establish controls to ensure timely liquidation of cash advances
such as requiring complete documentary requirements not only upon
liquidation but also upon granting of the same to establish accountability.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to
reconcile the accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds and draw the
necessary journal entry to correct the balances.

Recommendations
We recommended, and Management agreed to the following actions:
The MHRMO:
Prepare a Master list of earned leave credits of the officials and employees of the LGU as of December 31, 2024, and submit to the Local Finance Committee thru the Office of the MBO for inclusion in future budget appropriations until all earned leave credits are fully covered by appropriation and available funds;
Prepare quarterly reports of leave credits earned of all officials and employees, starting CY 2025, indicating their peso equivalents and supported individual leave cards; and
Submit the Masterlist and the quarterly reports to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books, subject to certification of the availability of appropriations and funds certified by the MBO and Municipal Treasurer (MT).
For the Municipal Accountant, upon receipt of the Masterlist and quarterly reports, record the corresponding liability in the books, supported by  certification from the MBO and MT on the availability of appropriations and funds.

Recommendations
We recommended, and Management agreed the following:
The Municipal Accountant:
Record all purchases of supplies and materials as inventory, except for those paid from reimbursement and/or petty cash fund in compliance with Section 114 of NGAS for LGUs;
Through the Office of the Municipal Mayor, issue a memorandum to all end-users mandating the timely submission of SSMI weekly, and FCR monthly; and
The Inventory Committee:
Conduct a physical count of supplies and materials semi-annually; and
Submit the RPCI to both the Municipal Accountant and GSO for reconciliation.

Recommendations
We recommended, and Management agreed to the following:
The Appraisal and Disposal Committee:
Convene and initiate the disposal and appraisal process for unserviceable properties by preparing the IIRUP with the supporting documents, in accordance with COA – Department of Budget and Management (DBM) JC 2024-01;
Submit IIRUP and relevant documents on demolished PPE to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books, copy furnished COA; and
Ensure attendance in the CY 2025 COA Seminar on Disposal of Government Properties to enhance capacity on proper disposal procedures.
The Municipal Accountant:
Draw the JEV to derecognize demolished PPE upon receipt of the  IIRUP with supporting documents;
Reclassify misclassified PPE items to their proper accounts and prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries; and
Provide a disclosure in the NTFS on the existence of impaired assets in compliance with IPSAS 1 and the action to be undertaken thereon.

Recommendations
We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to perform the following:
a. Prepare a JEV to reclassify the affected claims to its proper account classification using the herein PAJE; and
Accounts Payable				₱811,900.21
Customers’ Deposit Payable		   233,761.00
Leave Benefits Payable				₱1,045,661.21
To reclassify the terminal leave monetary claim and market stall deposits to its appropriate account
Provide a disclosure in the NTFS on the nature and amount of the terminal leave benefits payable.

Recommendations
We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
Take up the BFP downloaded funds in the amount of ₱43,255.00 in the books of account;
Coordinate with the MTO to notify the payees of stale checks totaling ₱42,527.90 and prepare the corresponding JEV to revert the amount to CIB-LCCA, supported by the list of stale checks: and
Ensure the timely preparation and submission of the BRS for all bank accounts to reconcile book and bank balances, in consonance with IPSAS 1 and Section 74 of PD No. 1445.

Recommendations
We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed, to provide disclosure in the NTFS on the following:
The accounting policies adopted for the measurement of inventories, including the basis of cost measurement (e.g., FIFO, weighted average, etc.); and
A detailed loan amortization schedule for the NC-LP – Domestic shall be disclosed to present information on the loan terms, interest rates, maturity profiles, and repayment schedules, in compliance with IPSAS 12 and IPSAS 30.

Recommendation
Moving forward, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the required financial reports of the 20 per cent DF and loans as a supporting schedule to the GF TB in compliance with the requirement of Section 313 of RA 7160, to achieve its intended objectives, and to serve as a reliable basis in formulating informed and sound decisions.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed, that, moving forward, all disbursements not made through checks or cash will be covered with a DV, in full compliance with the provisions of Section 5.e of PD No. 1445 and Section 344 of RA No. 7160.

Recommendations
We recommended, and the LCE agreed to the following:
The MDRRM Officer to ensure that all disbursements from the 70 per cent MF are strictly aligned with the guidelines outlined in JMC No. 2013-1 and the approved AIP. Any identified ineligible activities should be reallocated to appropriate funding sources, such as the GF, or adjusted to comply with applicable regulations; and
Consider increasing the budget allocation for the administration and operations of the MDRRM Office in the GF, to avoid mischarging operational expenses to the MiF.

Recommendation
We recommended, and the LSB agreed, to strictly adhere to the prescribed procedures and ensure that the required approval by the majority of the LSB members present is obtained, in compliance with Section 100(b) of RA No. 7160.

Recommendation
12.9 We recommended, and the Gender and Development Focal Point System (GFPS) agreed, to undertake the following: (a) ensure the full implementation of the identified GAD PPAs by year-end; (b) provide the required MOVs in the GAD AR, specifically: a brief summary of each reported program or project; results of the HGDG tests, and documentation of actions taken by the LGU on COA audit findings and recommendations; and c) attend to trainings and seminars on GAD.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed to submit the necessary proof of settlement of the NS and follow up with the COA CP on the status of the appealed ND to secure a copy of the Resolution of the case, which will enable the issuance of the respective NSSDC.

We recommended that Municipal Management direct the Environment SWMO, together with the MPDC, to: a) Ensure compliance with the requirements of the EMB V and other related agencies to facilitate the completion and approval of the Solid Waste Management Plan and the development of the proposed sanitary landfill in the Municipality; b) Conduct continuing information, education, and communication (IEC) campaign to raise awareness about the pertinent provisions of R.A. 9003, particularly on waste segregation at source and waste diversion; c) Coordinate with the Barangay Ecological Solid Waste Management Council (BESWMC) to ensure that the Municipal Programs on solid waste management are properly communicated and observed at the barangay level; d) Require the barangays, through its BESWMC, to ensure that the mandate of Executive Order No. 06 and Executive Order No. 12, series of 2023, are strictly observed by the households and various institutions in the Municipality; and e) Direct the BESWMC to comply with the reporting requirements of the Municipality and other related agencies to facilitate the monitoring of its solid waste management activities and to timely communicate and address their concerns.

We recommend that Management: a) Conduct a complete physical inventory of all the LGU properties and equipment to determine all the insurable properties for application of insurance policies with the GSIS, and if not approved, with any private insurance company in accordance with Section 5 of RA No. 656, for protection against any insurable risk; and b) Employ appropriate action to facilitate the immediate processing and application of insurance for all insurable LGU’s building structures by directing all concerned personnel to prepare and submit necessary documents as required in the GSIS.

We recommended that Management: a) Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete annual physical count, including assignment of property stickers to all property, plant, and equipment of the Municipality to prove their existence, determine the condition and the valuation, and prepare the RPCPPE; b) Implement the procedures prescribed under COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, in the conduct of physical count of PPE, recognition of properties that were physically counted during the conduct of physical count of PPE, disposition of non-existing/missing PPE items, and derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs without an available record of accountability, for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances of government agencies to produce a reliable report and PPE balances; c) Designate LGU personnel from the accounting and property offices who shall maintain and update the PPE Ledger/Property Cards and regularly reconcile their records, as well as maintain an updated ARE and safekeep all documents relative to the acquisition of properties; d) Direct the GSO to secure and ensure that the ARE are completely and correctly filled out with necessary details such as brand name, color, model, applicable serial number, and other important information about the property; e) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the Investment Property, Land account to Land account; f) Require the Inventory Committee to conduct an inventory of local roads, highways, and bridges within its area of jurisdiction which were acquired through purchase, construction, or donations; g) Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the GSO with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects; h) Direct the Municipal Accountant to record all road networks and their components, based on the Registry of Public Infrastructures, or in its absence, based on the complete Local Road Inventory Report provided with a complete description and cost segregation of the road components; i) Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Local Road Network Ledger Card in the prescribed form shown in Annex D of COA Circular No. 2015-008; and j) Direct the GSO to maintain an updated Local Road Inventory and Road Map and keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card of all roads within its area of jurisdiction.

We recommended that Management: a) Fully utilize the funds granted by the national agencies and implement the projects to effectively and timely extend to the constituents the benefits of the agency’s projects; b) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the return of the unutilized portion of the completed project/s to their respective source agencies so that the account of the LGU in the books of the source agencies is settled; and c) Expedite the implementation of the projects for which funds had already been made available since prior years.

We recommended that Management: a) Ensure compliance with the statutory appropriation requirement for LDRRMF, which mandates allocating not less than five percent of estimated revenue from regular sources of the LGU, to obtain sufficient funds to carry out the disaster risk management activities and attain the objective of RA No. 10121; b) Include and specify the list of PPAs to be charged against the LDRRM Fund, thus unexpended LDRRMF of previous years be listed in its PPAs as LDRRMFIP prior to the supplemental utilization of the same; c) Prepare and submit the required financial and other reports within the prescribed period to ensure the availability of information on the receipt of DRRM funds, as well as disaster relief aid/donations in cash and in-kind, to all stakeholders; and d) Strictly adhere to the state policy on DRRM by giving priority to the full implementation of planned PPAs to achieve the objectives/goals of the government in a disaster-resilient environment.

We recommended that Management: a) Ensure that the annual PPAs for GAD are achievable, meticulously planned, and regularly assessed and evaluated to ensure success and impact of the LGU Gender Development efforts; b) Revisit the internal policies and procedures on GAD and implement a well-defined monitoring and evaluation system to properly assess GAD PPAs’ responsiveness to gender mainstreaming and to avoid duplication of plans, programs; c) Instruct the GAD Focal Person/ GFPS to monitor the implementation of the identified PPAs and maximize utilization of the GAD funds to accomplish the purposes/objectives for which the GAD PPAs were identified to deliver immediate and optimum benefits to target beneficiaries; d) Direct the GAD Focal Point Chairperson to prepare GAD AR with actual GAD-related accomplishments and a more comprehensive or concrete indication of the effect of the implemented GAD PPAs and containing other appropriate and adequate information, as well as attach the required supporting documents; e) Implement a defined monitoring and evaluation system to properly assess the status and impact of GAD PPAs on gender mainstreaming; and f) Endorse the designated GFPS to GAD-related training for necessary knowledge and skills in heading GAD budgeting, implementation, and reporting.
We recommended that Management direct the Office Accounting to reconcile the accounts and draw the necessary journal entry to correct the balances.

We recommended that Management: a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to comply with Section 141 of the NGAS Manual for LGU, Volume III, which states that the recording of liabilities should arise only from goods delivered or services rendered, and make the necessary adjustments for those payables not covered with valid claim; and b) Send confirmation letters to creditors to prove the existence of these liability accounts and ensure that claims are substantiated with complete documentation; otherwise, revert said balances to Unappropriated Surplus Account.

We recommended that Management: a) Direct the Municipal Development Council (MDC) to ensure that PPAs in the Local Development Plan are supported with the necessary in-depth study and planning documents before its inclusion in the Plan to warrant the possible implementation of the project within the budget year; b) Through the MDC, annually review the feasibility of implementing the PPAs in prior years to ensure that the plan is responsive to the needs of the community and guarantee that the fund is optimally utilized to support the chosen site; c) Direct the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Planning & Development Coordinator, and other Implementing Offices, to ensure the optimum utilization of the 20% Development fund through the completion and accomplishment of the priority projects identified in the Local Development Plan; and d) If the financial capacity of the LGU permits, consider appointing additional personnel to address the staffing needs of the Office of the Municipal Engineer.

We recommended that Management: a) Create an Inventory Committee to oversee and conduct a semi-annual physical count and annual physical count of the inventory items; b) Require the GSO to conduct timely counts of Inventory items in the presence of all members of the Inventory Committee based on the timing prescribed by the Commission, and ensure proper and complete documentation of the results of the count to facilitate reliable reconciliation between the GSO records and the Accounting Office records; c) Direct the GSO to submit to the Accounting Department the corresponding report of receipt and issuances of inventories for proper and timely recording in the books of accounts; d) Direct the concerned offices to provide the necessary utilization reports, particularly for the long outstanding inventories, to the Office of the Municipal Accountant and subsequently, the latter review the details of the affected accounts, determine the status of the related inventories, and make appropriate adjustments, if warranted; e) Ensure the preparation of RPCI, SSMI, and SCs to facilitate reliable reconciliation between accounting and property records; f) Require the coordination between the Municipal Accountant and the GSO towards the implementation of the Perpetual Inventory Method in the recording of supplies and materials and maintaining the prescribed records to ensure proper accounting and promote better control in the receipt and issuance of supplies; g) To provide, if possible, a secured storage room for the supplies and materials of the LGU; h) Coordinate with the concerned departments/offices and develop internal control procedures that will ensure proper management of inventories to prevent or minimize the risk of loss and misuse; and i) Ensure compliance with the provisions of Republic Act No. 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations and Section 122 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs for the procurement of supplies and materials.

We recommended that Management: a) Ensure that the pertinent documents relating to the mandatory deductions such as the BIR withholding certificates are duly submitted by LGU personnel, particularly those who are newly employed; b) Coordinate with the concerned government agencies to facilitate the reconciliation and settlement of the discrepancies in the data information and/or pertinent records of the personnel/employees; c) Direct the AAO to closely coordinate with the Electronic Remittance File Officer on the resolution of the RBIs and the preparation and transmittal of the appropriate membership updating forms to GSIS before the following month’s remittance; d) Ensure that the remittances correspond to the exact amount withheld; and e) Deduct and remit amounts of mandatory counterpart and premium contributions, as well as loan repayments, within the prescribed period to avoid penalties and to guarantee that the employees enjoy the full benefits from GSIS, Pag-IBIG, and PhilHealth.

We recommended that Management: a) Direct the Accountable Officers (AOs), through the issuance of a demand letter, to submit liquidation report together with the supporting documents, to settle his/her cash advance; otherwise, require the immediate return of the amount of cash advance that remained unliquidated or cause the withholding of the payment of the AO’s salary; b) Coordinate with other government agencies to which the accountable officers are currently employed or connected to immediately settle their cash advances; c) Stop the grant of additional cash advances to personnel with unliquidated cash advances until such time previous cash advances are fully settled; d) Send demand letters to the accountable officers previously connected with the LGU who still unliquidated cash advances have, otherwise officials responsible for clearing said individuals from money accountability be held liable for the unsettled cash advances; and e) Monitor and establish controls to ensure timely liquidation of cash advances such as requiring complete documentary requirements not only upon liquidation but also upon granting of the same to establish accountability.

We recommended that Management: a) Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to submit the Statement of Itemized POP of the LGU relating to this cash advance where the computed duly certified by her; and b) Strictly comply with the provisions of Joint Circular No. 2015-01 for the entitlement, release, use, reporting, and audit of Confidential and/or Intelligence Funds.

We recommended that Management: a) Adopt policies and internal controls to monitor loan accommodations and other payroll deductions such that the monthly net take-home pay of an LGU personnel does not fall below the mandated minimum amount of ₱5,000.00; b) Direct the Agency Authorized Officer to coordinate with the person in charge of payroll preparation to exercise diligence in monitoring the net take-home pay of the LGU employees to comply with the requirements of existing law; c) Formulate and adopt remedial measures to rectify the situation of those LGU personnel receiving lower than the required minimum amount of net take-home pay; and d) Strictly implement the mandatory monthly net take-home pay of an amount not lower than P5,000.00.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to derecognize the cost of the fire truck including its corresponding accumulated depreciation in their books of accounts.

Recommendations
We recommended, and Management agreed to require the:
Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor to:
Determine the delinquent tax payers with tax due on 2018 and prior years which are recorded as receivables in the financial statements and provide the same with allowance for impairment;
Prepare aging of RPT/SET Receivables and regularly monitor the same and in order to provide allowance for impairment on receivables aged more than five years by a debit to Deferred RPT/SET Income and credit to Allowance for Impairment; and
Municipal Treasurer
To continuously post notices of delinquency for all delinquent tax payers or conduct information drive; and
In coordination with the Provincial Government, enforce and execute remedies prescribed in RA 7160 such as issuance of warrants of levy for receivables from 2019-2023.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV supported by appropriate documents to record and adjust the reconciling items in the books and ensure that reconciling items are timely adjusted to generate accurate financial reporting as of a given period.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to review and ensure that all disbursements are recorded in its appropriate account to avoid providing misleading information in the FS.

Recommendations
We recommended, and Management agreed to direct:
The Municipal Accountant in coordination with the MHO to make representation to the PhilHealth that the LGU will follow the accounting principle in recognizing revenue under IPSAS 9 on the amount received from PhilHealth for services rendered to patients with PhilHealth coverage;
The Accounting Office to:
Reclassify to Due to GOCC account the balance of funds received from PHIC which was recorded under Due to NGA and finally to income account;
moving forward record revenue and receivable upon rendering the service supported by List of Reimbursable Claims filed with PHIC and avoid accounting the receipt of payments from PHIC as TF; and
The MHO who will prepare, submit and monitor all the claims to PHIC, to furnish regularly the Accounting Office List of Reimbursable Claims from services rendered for timely recognition of revenue.

Recommendations
We reiterated our recommendation, and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Continue the reconciliation by retracing the causes of difference and prepare the reconciliation report of the reciprocal accounts;
After the reconciliation are completed and the difference is substantiated, draw a journal entry voucher to effect adjusting entries, and
Moving forward, record and set-up simultaneously the reciprocal accounts and constantly review and analyse the transactions before these are recorded.

Recommendations
We recommended, and Management agreed to direct:
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to secure and retrieve pertinent records and documents of the donated two construction and heavy equipment;
The Inventory committee, if securing of the pertinent records is not feasible, as an alternative, the amount to be used in recording may be assigned by the Inventory Committee based on the market/fair value of the item or similar item in the RPCPPE, if market/fair value could not be easily determined by the Inventory Committee, their fair value shall be determined by appraisal; and
The GSO in coordination with the Municipal Assessor to make representation to the:
BOL to verify and clarify the ownership of the ten (10) land lots owned by the Municipality as per tax declaration;
DENR for the possible creation of Memorandum of Agreement allowing the Municipality to use the land as cemetery considering that income was already derived therefrom and to also avoid unnecessary sanctions.

Recommendations
We recommended, and Management agreed to direct:
The BAC to require the winning bidder to post performance security in the prescribed amount and within ten (10) days (or when Section 37.1 of the RIRR allows a longer period) from the date the NOA was received and prior to entering into contract; and
The Municipal Accountant to require the suppliers or contractors to post warranty security in the amount and form provided under Section 62.1 par. two and 62.2.3.3 of the RIRR of RA No. 9184 prior to payment, to ensure that risk against manufacturing or structural defects are recovered therefrom. Otherwise, defects and failure within the warranty period not repaired by the suppliers or contractors shall be borne by the persons liable.

Recommendations
We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant together in coordination with the BAC to:
Submit the minutes of pre-procurement and pre-bid conference on the paid contracts,
Ensure that all supporting documents to approved contracts are complete before payments; and
Strictly follow the prescribed process and documentation in its procurement activities in accordance with RA 9184 and its RIRR.

Recommendations
We recommended, and Management agreed direct the Municipal Engineer:
To compute for the negative slippage incurred by the contractor to date and implement the calibrated measures provided under Item four of the GPPB Circular No. 03-2019; and
If warranted, in coordination with the BAC impose liquidated damages in an amount equal to at least one tenth (1/10) of one (1) percent of the cost of the unperformed portion of the works for every day of delay and when it reaches ten percent (10%) of the total contract price, the LGU shall further terminate the contract, forfeit the performance security, take over the contract and resort to any of other alternative methods of procurement or allow the contractor to continue the works without prejudice to the continued imposition of liquidated damages until the works have been completed in accordance with Item 9 of the Annex “F” of the RIRR of RA 7160.

Recommendations
We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the coordination of MPDC, Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Engineer to:
Support with SB Resolution the contracts that will be entered into for which payments thereof chargeable against the CY 2023 20% EDF appropriation which were described in generic terms and in lumpsum appropriation, as enumerated above, moving forward;
Ensure that the prepared and approved local development and AIP submitted by the development council to the local finance committee contains specific proposed projects for inclusion in the AB, otherwise, ensure that contracts and disbursements to be charged in the appropriation describe in generic terms in the AIP and AB be supported with SB Resolution authorizing the charging thereof; and
Initiate the preparation of Program of Works to support the estimate of projects funded under the 20% Economic Development Plan before budget preparation.

Recommendations
We recommended, and Management agreed to direct:
The MHO to seek new approval from DOH for the utilization of the grant amounting to P100,000.00 and submit fund utilization report thereof otherwise return the same in accordance with the MOA;
The MAO to undertake another public bidding for the procurement of native chickens, otherwise resort to appropriate alternative procurement method if warranted; and
The Implementing Office to
Submit the MOA of the projects, programs and activities recorded as due to NGAs to the office of the auditor; and
Coordinate with the MA and MT for the status of the projects and funds, and determine if there is a need to return the unused balance to the source agency.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all payments made in the TF are in accordance to its specific purpose for which the fund is intended.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to provide accurate certifications on unexpended and unobligated DRRMF balance available for reappropriation.

Recommendation
Moving forward, we recommended, and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to transfer the unexpended current balance of the LDDRMF in the GF to TF by preparing disbursement voucher and the MT to draw a check and deposit the same to transfer at year-end instead of a mere set-up of obligation.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed to capacitate its GFPS on how to undertake gender analysis by sending them to training seminars and require the creation and maintenance of GAD database containing sex disaggregated data and other gender-related information which shall serve as basis in conducting gender analysis, identifying gender issues and determining the appropriate PAPs that would address the identified gender issues to be incorporated in the GAD Plan and Budget.

Recommendation

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Collectors to remit daily all collections to the Liquidating Officer; and the designated Liquidating Officer to deposit cash turned over by the Collectors daily and intact or not later than the next banking day.

We recommended that Management:
strictly  enforce the settlement of all unliquidated cash advances as of December 31, 2018 and/or implement the withholding of salary until such time the cash advances are settled in accordance with existing regulations;
stop the granting of additional cash advances to Accountable Officers, officials and employees unless the previous ones are fully settled, to avoid accumulation of the same; and
institute appropriate legal actions/sanctions against those erring accountable officers/employees for continued non-compliance with the existing laws, rules and regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, if warranted.

We recommended that Management require the Petty Cash Custodian to:
replenish the PCF when disbursements reach 75 per cent of the fund;
adopt the imprest system of disbursements (thru checks) for payment of AICS and/or thru grant of cash advances by the Disbursing Officer; and
adhere strictly on the guidelines on the grant of PCF per COA Circular No. 2012-001.

c)  the acting Municipal Accountant  to record the appropriate  accounts upon submission of the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and the appropriate supporting documents.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to return immediately the unexpended/unutilized balance of the fund transfers and/or make representation with the source agencies and request for authority to utilize the amount for related projects in the municipality.
We recommended that Management ensure that all projects identified under the 20% Economic Development Fund are fully implemented within the budget year.

We recommended that Management:
conduct regular monitoring of on-going private construction to ensure that appraisal and assessment on  buildings  are issued upon completion of construction;
conduct regular inspection of real properties to validate actual or predominant use of property to provide assurance that collections are based on correct, accurate and credible assessments; and
issue Notices of Tax Delinquency to those real properties with  no payment from 2017 and earlier years and post the notices in accordance with Section 254 of 1991 LGC, to optimize collection of RPT.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive directs:


     a. the Inventory Committee to complete/finish the conduct of  physical
        count of all the properties of the Municipality  to  ascertain  the
        existence of the assets and submit a report thereon to the  auditor
        in accordance with Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS, Volume I.  On
        the basis of the inventory  report,  determine  the  officials  and
        employees  to  whom  the  equipment  were  issued  and  issue   the
        corresponding AREs to pinpoint accountability.


     b. the Municipal  Accountant  and  the  General  Services  Officer  to
        maintain complete and updated PPE and Real  Property  ledger  cards
        and property cards, respectively, to  facilitate  inventory  taking
        and periodic reconciliation between  the  property  and  accounting
        records and arrive at the correct balances of the PPE accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  direct  the  Municipal
  Accountant to conduct a thorough review and  analysis  of  the  Property,
  Plant  and  Equipment   accounts   particularly   those   pertaining   to
  depreciation adopting the policy on residual value equivalent to at least
  five percent (5%) of the cost.   Likewise,  make  sure  that  an  updated
  lapsing  schedule  on  depreciation  is  completed  and   the   necessary
  corrections/adjustments  in  the  books  of  accounts  are  effected   in
  pursuance to PPSAS 17.

We recommended that the Accountant exerts  more  effort  to  look  for
   pertinent documents and records in order to justify the validity  of  the
   adjusting entries made. Supporting documents  like  copy  of  the  check,
   debit/credit memos, bank statement, check registers or any other records
   that will trace back and identify these unaccounted  reconciling  amounts
   are very important to  prove  also  the  validity  and  accuracy  of  the
   balances of the above affected accounts.


   We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal  Treasurer’s
   Office  and  Accounting  Office  be   directed   to   conduct   immediate
   reconciliation of their records in order to determine the causes  of  the
   discrepancy and come up with an accurate and reliable financial data,  in
   compliance with the above-cited regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  require  the  Municipal
   Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer  to  immediately  reconcile  their
   records to avoid further accumulation  of  errors/reconciling  items  and
   determine the nature of the  transactions  comprising  the  discrepancies
   between their records and thereafter,  prepare  the  necessary  adjusting
   entries.


      We further recommended that both the Accounting and  Treasury  offices
   reconcile their records of Cash Local  Treasury  at  least  quarterly  to
   ensure the correctness of their respective records.


We recommended that the Local Chief Executive requires:


     a. The officers/employees with outstanding cash advances for more than
        the allowable period of time or for  which  purposes  have  already
        been served,  to  settle  them  by  rendering  liquidation  reports
        thereon and refund whatever unused amount still in their hands.


     b. The Municipal Accountant to certify each and every claim  for  cash
        advance that  the  person  drawing  the  such  has  no  outstanding
        previous cash advances prior to processing the claim


      We also recommended that the  Municipal  Accountant  closely  monitors
   outstanding advances of officials  and  employees  and  demand  immediate
   settlement pursuant to above cited relevant regulations.   If  warranted,
   withholding  of  salaries  of  those  who  failed  to  comply  with   the
   requirements must be imposed.


      We recommended further that Management consider filing a request  for
   authority to write-off the dormant unliquidated cash advance of the  late
   Nicomedes F. Patenia, former Municipal Mayor of the Municipality pursuant
   to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive directs  the  concerned
  Property and Supply Office to prepare a list of all insurable  properties
  of the Municipality and coordinate with the GSIS as  to  all  the  needed
  documents  to  facilitate  the  proper  and  immediate  application   for
  insurance of such properties.

      We also recommended also that the  funds  needed  for  the  insurance
  premiums be included in the annual budget of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive stop approving  payments
   of honoraria to personnel engaged in the Alternative Learning System,  if
   such are charged against the SEF, as this is not among the eligible
   expense chargeable thereto. Likewise, strict adherence to Section 1.1 of
   Letter of Instructions  No.  1462,  on  the  prioritization  of  expenses
   chargeable to SEF is enjoined.

We recommended that the Municipality discontinue charging against  the
   Special Education Fund the fuel  expenses  of  privately  owned  vehicles
   pursuant to Item 2 of Section 4 of PD 1445.


      We also recommended that all pertinent documents on the said purchased
   and donated motor vehicles should be produced  and  properly  coordinated
   with the donor agency so that the same be transferred under the  name  of
   the Municipality/School and be recorded in the books.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  directs  the  concerned
   Municipal  Officials  to  monitor  the  timely   implementation   of
   developmental projects, programs and activities and see to  it  that
   the 20% development funds are optimally utilized in accordance  with
   the  guidelines  and  for the project/program prescribed in the Joint
   Memorandum Circular No. 1.s 2005 of the DILG and DBM as  amended  by
   Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated 13 April 2011.

We recommended that the Management should explain and  justify  the
   said payments of the agency’s loan amortization for  various  development
   projects charged against the 20% Development  Fund,  which  exceeded  the
   authorized debt service cap.


        Also, we recommended that the  management  should  comply  strictly
   with the guidelines provided for under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular
   No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

We reiterated our previous recommendation  that  the  Municipal  Mayor
   instructs the concerned official to  comply  strictly  with  any  of  the
   provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 to facilitate validation  of  the
   same by the Audit Team and to  promote  transparency  and  accountability
   regarding the PPAs undertaken by the Municipality.

We recommended that the BAC and other concerned offices  submit  all
   the required supporting documents enumerated above to the audit team  through
   the Accountant to attest to the propriety and  validity  of  the  subject
   transactions.


      We also recommended  that  the  Accountant  ensures  that  all  Agency
   financial   transactions   are   duly   supported   with    required
   documentations before processing the same for payment to avoid audit
   suspensions and/or audit disallowances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor directs the:


   a. BAC of the Municipality and all  those  involved  in  the  procurement
      process  to  strictly  comply  with  the  procedures  and  documentary
      requirements for government procurement prescribed in the  above  laws
      and regulations;


   b. the Accounting Office to strictly follow the guidelines prescribed  by
      RIRR 9184 and ensure the completeness of supporting documents for each
      procurement transaction as required by COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated
      June 14, 2012 and other pertinent  laws  and  government  regulations.
      Henceforth, process,  approve  and  pay  only  those  claims  with
      complete documentation.

We again recommendedthat Management:(1) create an Inventory Committee
that will conduct on a full-time basis the annual inventory of all property, plant and
equipment of the Municipality to prove their existence, determine the condition and
the valuation, and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and
Equipment (RPCPPE); and (2)directthe Municipal Accountant to update the PPE
Ledger Cards, and the acting GSO to also update the Property Cards and the
prompt renewal of all Acknowledgment Receipt for Equipment (ARE) pursuant to
existing regulations.

We further recommended that Management direct the Accounting and the
GSO of the Municipality to reconcile their records, in accordance with the
aforementioned regulations.

We recommended that Management:

a) strictly enforce the settlement of all unliquidated cash advances as of
December 31, 2016;

b) stop the granting of additional cash advances to Accountable Officers,
officials and employees unless the previous ones are fully settled, to avoid
accumulation of the same;

c) institute appropriate legal actions/sanctions against those erring
accountable officers/employees for continued non-compliance with the
existing laws, rules and regulations on the granting, utilization and
liquidation of cash advances, if warranted.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to
ensure completeness of supporting documents before a disbursement is certified
for payment and submit immediately the required supporting documents, to
forestall the issuance of Notice of Suspension.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to
ensure that expenditres are properly authorized by a Sanggunian Resolution, and
documented with the required supporting documents before certifying for payment.

We recommended that Management:

(a) require the Offices to evaluate their workforce versus functions to justify
the hiring of the needed JOs in the exigency of the service; and

(b) facilitate the recruitment and selection process of the applicants in
contracting the services of JOs employees.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to
examine the completeness of the supporting document for every transactions of the
municipal government before approving the same.

We recommended that Management:

a. ensure that only regular employees are allowed to attend workshop and
trainings/seminars which are relevant, mandatory and responsive to the
capacity-building needs or requirements of the LGU to avoid any wasteful
and inefficient utilization of fund; and

b. conduct workshops/trainings such as the Executive and Legislative Agenda
(ELA) within the Municipality or province to promote economy and efficient
utilization of public funds

We recommended that Management require the Supply Officer/Drivers to
accomplish adequate documentation to support the procurement of fuel, oil and
lubricants. We also recommended that Management strictly monitor the issuance of
withdrawal slips to avoid the incurrence of over withdrawal of gasoline.

We recommended that Management study the packages offered by mobile
networks and evaluate which would provide the best price favorable to the local
government. We likewise recommend that Management refrain from paying
excessive expenditures as required under COA Circular No. 2012-003 and see to it
that claims are supported with adequate documentation.

We recommended that Management require the Local School Board to
utilize the proceeds of the SEF only for such purposes prescribed by law, otherwise
expenditures shall be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that Management stop granting monthly mobile load
allowance to Public School Principals and District Snpervisors in the Municipality.
We likewise recommend that Management refrain from paying unmecessary
expenditures as required under COA Circular No. 2012-003.

We recommended that Management institute appropriate measures to
require the contractor to operationalize the automation project otherwise rescind
the contract, forfeit the performance hond and bold him liable for any expenses that maybe
incurred in the process including the payment of bank interests/charges until such
time that the project becomes operational.

We recommended that Management institute appropriate measures to claim
liguidated damages for failure to deliver the project on time and assert forfeiture of
the performance bond.
